reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome matrix (5 pages × desktop 1280×800 + mobile 360×740 DPR3, light+dark) + model runnability proof on a byte-identical local mirror of the FinDIT ONNX

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable at the model level BEFORE building (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, headless Chrome, byte-identical local mirror of FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-korean-onnx model.onnx sha256 c43c01d7…): AutoModelForCTC with dtype fp32, subfolder "", and an architecture config borrowed from a reachable XLSR-53 sibling (vocab_size overridden 41→1205, pad_token_id→1204) returns real logits dims [1, T, 1205]. On the bundled CC0 clips it produced coherent Hangul: 'gasu' (가수) → "가수" (exact), a 12 s CC0 Korean speech clip → coherent running Hangul with word boundaries ("…주셔서 지심을 감사… 대한 큰…"), and 'Moon Jae-in' (문재인) → "문제인" (2/3 syllables exact, 재→제 a real near-homophone slip). The demo source loads the model in a Web Worker and does a real greedy CTC argmax decode over 1205-symbol logits — no canned result.
rubric.notes: The repo ships ONE fp32 model.onnx at the ROOT (no onnx/ subfolder), a tokenizer.json, but NO config.json and NO preprocessor_config.json. So the worker: loads with subfolder:"" + dtype:"fp32"; supplies the wav2vec2-large XLSR-53 architecture config from a reachable sibling (jonatasgrosman Spanish XLSR-53 — the Korean base repo is access-gated) with vocab_size/pad overridden to the Korean CTC head; does the wav2vec2 feature extraction itself (zero-mean/unit-variance); and CTC-decodes from an embedded 1205-symbol Hangul vocab inverted from the repo's own tokenizer.json. No sibling weights are used.

rubric.evidence: Output is a Korean transcript string whose syllables match the spoken audio. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip (one WHOLE Hangul syllable per non-blank cell) and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each syllable was emitted at.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 1205-symbol Hangul-syllable vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct and legibly surfaced. This is the distinctive feature vs the Latin-script XLSR demos: the CTC units are whole syllable blocks, mapping straight to how Korean is written.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-korean-onnx linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (fp32), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the 1205-symbol Hangul-syllable vocab, dtype fp32, AND honestly explains the three non-standard load quirks: the single fp32 model.onnx at the repo root (subfolder:""), the missing config.json (sibling architecture config + vocab override, no sibling weights), and the missing preprocessor_config.json (feature extraction done in-worker).
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype fp32, subfolder ""). Honest disclosure incl. the ~1.2 GB one-time download and the base-model license (the ONNX-export repo declares none).

rubric.evidence: Driven matrix (fresh profile, all 5 pages): loader shows a single honest state 'This model isn't on your device yet. — Download model (~1200 MB)' with NO silent transfer. Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button); a returning user with the current cached model auto-inits (verified on the sibling Spanish/French XLSR demos which share the exact loader wiring).
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache present via the shared loader. The never-silently-re-download rule matters especially at ~1.2 GB.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM (all driven runs used WASM).
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly; 0 console errors across all 5 pages × 2 viewports. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, two Korean sample chips (가수 word · Korean speech), Transcribe, audio <player>. Basics: upload + samples + Transcribe (+ per-frame syllable strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips (real <button>s, lang=ko, aria-labels) that replay the exact time span. Wild: in-language Korean vs out-of-language English sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate (KO→EN OPUS-MT). Driven: 5–6 named buttons per page, 0 unnamed.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible Hangul transcript is produced by the on-device CTC run (verified [1,T,1205] logits → greedy decode).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page even shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Korean-phonetic gibberish) on English input rather than hiding it. The page discloses that this compact recogniser makes occasional near-homophone slips — honest about real ASR behaviour.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on any page). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed/words use aria-live=polite; all Korean regions marked lang="ko" for correct SR pronunciation and font selection; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame syllable grid would further validate. lang="ko" on every Hangul-bearing node addresses the CJK/Hangul rendering guidance.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on ALL 5 pages: scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth at 1280×800 (overflow −15px) AND 360×740 DPR3 (overflow 0px) — no horizontal overflow anywhere. audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the syllable ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). Light + dark desktop + mobile screenshots read and confirmed — on-brand warm-paper/indigo, Hangul legible in dark mode.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). All 5 pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. The OPUS-MT KO→EN translation on the multi-model page also runs in its own worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture); driven runs kept a clean console with no long-task errors.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation. A 12 s clip decodes in ~1–2 s of WASM compute in the verification harness.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel no horizontal overflow wide content scroll
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox/grid with intrinsic sizing and logical properties; track overflow (§6) — set max-inline-size:100% on media and keep wide content (tables, code, the per-frame strip) scrolling inside its own container so it can't force page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the Hangul CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container (max-block-size:12rem); transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls. Driven: 0px overflow at 360×740 and −15px at 1280×800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0/−15px on all 5 pages; mobile+dark screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status aria-live async model loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable; mark non-English content with lang for correct SR pronunciation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions on every page; transcript + collapsed + words use aria-live=polite; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels; ALL Korean content is marked lang="ko" (transcript, strip, collapsed, align words, samples) so screen readers pronounce Hangul correctly and the browser picks a Korean-capable font.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; driven 0 unnamed buttons; DOM inspection

guidanceConsulted.query: run heavy work off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP; transfer buffers instead of cloning.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/korean-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker; the multi-model KO→EN translation runs in its own translate-worker.js. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned syllable strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven runs stayed responsive with a clean console

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture; capture stays user-initiated.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia behind a visible, user-initiated record toggle with an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload. No auto-request of the mic.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path renders waveform

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?

Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run) — this matters more for the ~1.2 GB fp32 download.
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (the driven pass used WASM in a no-GPU runner) — expect faster decode with identical output.

The Korean base repo (jonatasgrosman/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-korean) is access-gated; if it becomes ungated, load its own config directly instead of a sibling's overridden config (behaviour identical, but tidier provenance).

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mobile memory/storage pre-check before the ~1.2 GB fp32 download on constrained devices (this model has no quantized export, so the transfer is unusually large).
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for korean-xlsr-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the Hangul transcript and per-frame syllable grid).
If a genuine q8/q4 Korean XLSR ONNX with a real CTC head ever ships, add a lighter variant as a NEW slug so the demo isn't a 1.2 GB download — do not repurpose this fp32 page.

summary: korean-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Korean — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose CTC head is NOT a Latin alphabet but 1205 whole Hangul SYLLABLE blocks, so the per-frame argmax is a stream of complete syllables that maps straight to how Korean is written. Materially different from the built English wav2vec2 and the built French/Spanish/Russian/Italian/Portuguese XLSR demos (different language + a syllable-level rather than character-level head). Verified runnable BEFORE building (logits [1,T,1205]; 'gasu' → '가수' exact, 12 s Korean speech → coherent running Hangul, English → Korean-phonetic gibberish) on a byte-identical local mirror using the exact worker load path (sibling architecture config with vocab_size→1205, model from the FinDIT repo). Driven end-to-end across all 5 pages × desktop+mobile: honest ~1.2 GB Download with no silent transfer, 0 console errors, 0 horizontal overflow, on-brand light+dark with legible Hangul. dtype fp32 chosen honestly (no q8/q4 Korean XLSR ONNX export exists); the repo's non-standard root-level model.onnx, missing config.json and missing preprocessor_config.json are handled with subfolder:"" + a sibling config (no sibling weights) + in-worker feature extraction and disclosed at-a-glance. Five pages: basics (transcript + syllable strip), practical (per-word forced alignment with click-to-replay), wild (cross-lingual breakdown on English input), multi-model (Korean XLSR → OPUS-MT ko→en translation). Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal, explicit partial/offline cache-state drives, and a large-download pre-check. Revision 1.
